metadata.title: Letter regarding admission of Karoon Gengradomying, 1941-09-08
metadata.creator: anonymous
metadata.description: A letter from Miami University addressing the admission of Karoon Gengradomying as a freshman, mentioning other Thai students at the university.
metadata.transcript:
[HANDWRITTEN: September 8, 1941]

Mr. Murray Sheehan
Monsoonhurst 59
Glouchester, Massachusetts

Dear Murray:

Replying to your letter of September 6, I can
assure you that we shall be pleased to admit Karoon
Gengradomying to Miami University as a freshman.

While of course we know nothing of the University
you mention in Bangkok or of its entrance examinations,
the other boys from Thailand have made such a good
record here that we do not hesitate.

Freshman Week begins with a convocation next
Sunday evening, September 14, but we can hold a room
for him a little longer if necessary. We are trying
to squeeze him into one of the freshman dormitories.

Cordially yours,

President
metadata.text_reading: September 8, 1941 Mr. Murray Sheehan Monsoonhurst 59 Glouchester, Massachusetts Dear Murray: Replying to your letter of September 6, I can assure you that we shall be pleased to admit Karoon Gengradomying to Miami University as a freshman. While of course we know nothing of the University you mention in Bangkok or of its entrance examinations, the other boys from Thailand have made such a good record here that we do not hesitate. Freshman Week begins with a convocation next Sunday evening, September 14, but we can hold a room for him a little longer if necessary. We are trying to squeeze him into one of the freshman dormitories. Cordially yours, President
